team.name: customer_support
team.purpose: Handle all customer service related issues, complaints and feedback

team.name: corporate_clients
team.header: Team dealing with corporate client relations
team.purpose: Handle all issues, feedback and communication related to corporate clients

channel.name: virtual_appointments
channel.header: Channel for issues related to virtual appointments
channel.purpose: Discuss and resolve issues related to booking and availability of virtual appointments

channel.name: client_feedback
channel.header: Channel for discussing feedback from corporate clients
channel.purpose: Analyze client feedback and develop strategies for improvement

post.message: Good morning team, please remember we have a meeting at 2pm to discuss the new updates on our digital platform.
Thanks for the reminder, Rachel. I'll make sure to have my notes ready for the meeting.
Team, we've just received some interesting feedback from one of our corporate clients. Let's discuss this in our meeting next week.
John, could you please take the lead on summarizing last month's customer satisfaction surveys? We need to present it in the quarterly review.
Rachel, could you share with us the latest stats on customer support requests? We need it for the client portfolio update.
Sure Rachel, I'll prepare the survey summary for the review. Let's aim to improve our satisfaction rates next quarter!

Hello team, I just finished compiling the 2024 annual reviews from our corporate clients. I noticed a common issue raised by 73% of them. They've requested that we improve appointment availability for their employees. HR managers have reported that 44% of their employees who attempted to book virtual consultations during work hours couldn't find available slots within a 48-hour window. This is definitely something we need to address.

A further concern is that two of our enterprise clients, representing about 1,200 employees, have threatened to explore alternative virtual healthcare providers due to these access issues. We could potentially lose a significant portion of our user base if we don't take action.

Thank you for sharing this, Jane. This indeed sounds concerning and is definitely something we need to prioritize. We should look into enhancing our patient communication technology to streamline the booking process. A more efficient system could potentially reduce wait times and increase patient satisfaction and retention.
